headline: Treasury's RM38 billion special purpose vehicle escapes parliamentary scrutiny
context: The Treasury established a RM38 billion special purpose vehicle for infrastructure funding that operates outside normal parliamentary appropriation and audit processes.

cards.big: RM38 billion infrastructure fund operates outside Parliament's budget oversight
cards.sub: Special purpose vehicle created to bypass normal appropriation controls

cards.big: SPV incorporated under Companies Act — not subject to parliamentary supply bill process
cards.sub: Government created a corporate entity to spend public money without legislative approval

cards.big: SPV borrows against government guarantee — liabilities ultimately fall on taxpayers
cards.sub: Off-balance-sheet spending hides true size of government fiscal commitments

cards.big: AG audited SPV only after PAC formal request — not through standard annual audit
cards.sub: Oversight is reactive not systematic — requiring political will to trigger review

cards.big: Moving money off the books doesn't make it disappear — it just makes it invisible to Parliament
Should all government SPVs be subject to the same audit and reporting as ministries?
